scope_note: QA Witt Tower General v_p Period Law cert [439]. Unifies [437] (r=1) and [438] (r=2) into a single law covering ALL v_p(t-2)=r>=1. For M=[[t,-1],[1,0]] (det=+1 companion), p prime, v_p(t-2)=r exactly (p^r|(t-2), p^(r+1) does not), c_r=(t-2)/p^r with gcd(c_r,p)=1, the orbit-count distribution on (Z/p^k Z)^2 is: [k<=r]: count(1)=p^k; count(p^L)=(p-1)*p^(k-1) for L=1..k (all layers joint birth -- k unsplit layers, fixed-point count grows with k). [k>r]: count(1)=p^r (SATURATION -- fixed-point count caps at p^r once k passes r); count(p^L)=(p^2-1)*p^(L+r-2) for L=1..k-r (frozen -- same as [437]/[438] but exponent shifted by r-1); count(p^L)=(p-1)*p^(k-1) for L=k-r+1..k (joint birth -- r layers, one per unit of ramification). ALGEBRAIC MECHANISM (exact): N=M-I satisfies N^2=(t-2)*M=p^r*c_r*M (exact integer identity). Fixed-point condition Nx=0 mod p^k reduces to a=b and c_r*a=0 mod p^(k-r), giving a=b and a in p^max(k-r,0)*Z/p^k Z. Therefore ker(N mod p^k) = {(a,a): a in Z/p^k Z, a=0 mod p^max(k-r,0)} = p^min(r,k) elements. This is the COMPLETE MECHANISM: the r units of p-adic ramification each contribute one free lifting step; once k>r, further lifting is constrained. UNIFIED RECOVERY: setting r=1 recovers [437] formula identically (frozen=(p^2-1)*p^(L-1), 1 birth layer); setting r=2 recovers [438] formula identically (frozen=(p^2-1)*p^L, 2 joint birth layers). EXCEPTION: p=3, r=1, c=t-2)/p ≡ p-1 mod p (p=3 stall documented in [437]) -- different orbit splitting at k=2; all other (p,r,c_r) follow the unified law. (C1) GEN_PERIOD_SET: period set={p^L:L=0..k} for r=3, p=3,5, k=1..5; zero violations PASS. (C2) GEN_FIXED_SATURATION: count(1)=p^min(r,k); p=3,5,7, r=1..4, c_r=1..2, k=1..5; zero failures PASS. (C3) GEN_UNIFIED_FORMULA: full orbit law, p=3 r=1..3 k=1..5, p=5 r=1..3 k=1..4, p=7 r=1..2 k=1..3 (excluding p=3 stall); zero violations PASS. (C4) ALGEBRAIC_KER: ker(N mod p^k)=p^min(r,k) verified t=3..200, p=3 k<=4, p=5,7 k<=3; zero failures PASS. (C5) RECOVERS_437: r=1 formula = [437] closed form for all p=3,5,7 c_r=1..p-1 k=1..4; PASS. (C6) RECOVERS_438: r=2 formula = [438] closed form for all p=3,5,7 c_2=1..p-1 k=1..4; PASS. WHY NOT DUPLICATE [437]/[438]: those certs establish the r=1 and r=2 cases; this cert proves the general r law and establishes the mechanism ker(N)=p^min(r,k) that explains ALL previous cases. THEOREM NT: all arithmetic exact integer (mod p^k or exact identity N^2=(t-2)M); no float, no observer projection. 6 checks PASS; 7/7 fixtures PASS; self-test ok. Primary sources: Serre, J.-P. (1979) 'Local Fields' doi:10.1007/978-1-4757-5673-9 (ramification groups, higher ramification, Witt vectors); Ireland, K. and Rosen, M. (1990) 'A Classical Introduction to Modern Number Theory' ISBN 978-0-387-97329-6 Ch.7 (Hensel lifting, nilpotent elements mod p^k); Wall, D.D. (1960) American Mathematical Monthly 67(6) doi:10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Pisano-period structure for quadratic recurrences). Derived 2026-06-17.
